title: Decide: text-only updates remain as-is, video updates extend the same row
description:
Architecture decision bead — record the rationale once.

Decision: extend user_updates with nullable recording_* columns rather than a separate user_recordings table. Reasons:
- Reactions, unread tracking, sort order, and notifications already plug into user_updates; reusing keeps the UI surface unchanged.
- A user posting a hybrid (text + video) update is natural; two-table model would require either duplicate rows or a join on every read.
- Single Realtime subscription continues to work.

This bead exists so future-self can find the rationale in the bead history without re-deriving it. No code changes.

title: Unit: slug collision handling
description:
vitest. Mock a slug collision check function. Inputs:
- 'Hello World' -> 'hello-world'
- 'Hello World' (already exists) -> 'hello-world-2'
- 'Café résumé' -> 'cafe-resume'
- 'A'.repeat(100) truncates to <= 60 chars

title: Migration: rooms + room_members tables + RLS + Realtime
description:
supabase/migrations/<ts>_rooms.sql:
CREATE TABLE rooms (
  id uuid PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  name text NOT NULL CHECK (length(name) BETWEEN 1 AND 60),
  slug text UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  description text,
  theme_id text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'default',
  num_chairs int NOT NULL DEFAULT 15 CHECK (num_chairs BETWEEN 4 AND 30),
  visibility text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'invite' CHECK (visibility IN ('public','invite','private')),
  created_by uuid REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE TABLE room_members (
  room_id uuid REFERENCES rooms(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  user_id uuid REFERENCES users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  role text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'member' CHECK (role IN ('owner','admin','member')),
  joined_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(),
  PRIMARY KEY (room_id, user_id)
);
ALTER TABLE rooms ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE room_members ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE rooms;
ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE room_members;

RLS:
- rooms SELECT: visibility = 'public' OR EXISTS (room_members where user_id = auth.uid())
- rooms INSERT: auth.uid() IS NOT NULL (creator becomes owner via trigger)
- rooms UPDATE/DELETE: caller is owner/admin in room_members
- room_members SELECT: caller is in same room
- room_members INSERT: public room = self-insert; invite/private = caller is admin/owner
- room_members DELETE: self OR admin/owner

title: Playwright + 'pnpm test:e2e' setup
description:
Add @playwright/test devDependency. playwright.config.ts: webServer = { command: 'npm run dev', port: 5173 }. Two browser projects (chromium-A, chromium-B) for two-user fixtures.

Documents in README: setup, run, debugging.

title: Wire LiveKit ActiveSpeakers into User.speakingLevel (avatar pulse)
description: RoomEvent.ActiveSpeakersChanged fires with [{ identity, audioLevel }]. Map identity -> userId, write audioLevel into User.speakingLevel. Existing Participants.tsx already scales the avatar by speakingLevel.

title: Unit: MediaRecorder state-machine mock
description:
Mock the MediaRecorder global. Assert <RecordUpdate> transitions:
- start -> recording state, dataavailable subscribed
- pause -> paused state
- resume -> recording state
- stop -> idle, blob assembled from chunks

Vitest + jsdom.

title: Test user fixtures / seed script for E2E
description:
Playwright tests need stable test users. scripts/seed-test-users.ts:
- Reads TEST_SUPABASE_URL / TEST_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE from env
- Idempotently creates test-a@local, test-b@local, test-c@local via supabase.auth.admin.createUser()
- Inserts matching public.users rows
- Adds them to the seeded room

Document test-runner setup in README.md.

title: Talking stick lifecycle: clear on room leave; reset on empty room
description:
Currently the stick lives in cohort-wide state. With per-room sticks (sbc-2x0), we need lifecycle rules:

- On user leaving a room: if user holds the stick, transfer to the longest-seated member; if the user is the last in the room, clear the stick.
- On room becoming empty: ensure stick state is null in DB so next entrant starts clean.
- On a previously-stuck stick (user disconnected without clean leave): a 5-minute idle timer in client + server-side cleanup via a Postgres scheduled function.

Touch: src/store/useStore.ts passStick + leaveRoom + presence-departure handlers.

title: Migration: extend user_updates with recording_url + duration + thumbnail
description:
ALTER TABLE user_updates
  ADD COLUMN recording_url text,
  ADD COLUMN recording_duration_seconds int CHECK (recording_duration_seconds IS NULL OR recording_duration_seconds BETWEEN 1 AND 300),
  ADD COLUMN recording_thumbnail_url text;

All nullable: text-only updates remain valid. Update src/lib/database.types.ts and src/types/index.ts UserUpdate interface.

title: Recording UI: countdown, pause/resume, re-record, preview
description: Wraps <RecordUpdate>. UI states: idle -> recording (with elapsed time + 90s cap countdown) -> preview (loops the blob in a <video controls>) -> upload | re-record. Pause/resume buttons during recording.

title: Unit tests: signaling channel reuse + ready gating
description:
Mock @supabase/supabase-js channel + send. Assert:
- setupSignaling subscribes exactly once
- all three send paths use the same channel instance (object identity)
- send() called before SUBSCRIBED is queued/awaited, not dropped
- disconnect() calls removeChannel exactly once

Use vitest. Add npm script test:unit if absent.

title: Add 'import type { User }' to src/components/Participants.tsx
GitHub Actions CI: lint + typecheck + build on PR
description:
.github/workflows/ci.yml. Trigger: pull_request, push to main. Steps: checkout, setup-node 20, npm ci, npm run lint, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build.

Fails fast on the bugs we just fixed (e.g. sbc-1x9 missing import). Required check on PRs.

Optional follow-up: add E2E job that runs Playwright. Skip for now (slow + Supabase staging cost).

title: 'Create room' modal
description:
Modal triggered from Lobby. Form: name (text), description (textarea), theme picker (visual swatches from registry), num_chairs slider (4-30), visibility radio (public/invite/private).

On submit: generate slug (B6_6), insert into rooms, then insert self as room_members owner. Redirect into the new room.

title: Update src/types/index.ts: add Room + RoomMember types
src/lib/logger.ts with namespaces
description:
Tiny logger. createLogger(namespace) returns { debug, info, warn, error } that prepends [namespace] and only logs when namespace is in VITE_DEBUG_LOG (comma-separated) or VITE_DEBUG_LOG === '*'.

Namespaces used elsewhere: webrtc, livekit, recording, signaling, room, lobby, presence, stick.

Replace existing console.* calls in webrtc.ts as part of B1_5.

title: Manual smoke: TURN-only connectivity verification
description:
Document in docs/SMOKE.md the procedure:
1. Set VITE_FORCE_RELAY=true
2. Two devices on different networks (or one device + a phone hotspot)
3. Confirm peers connect; confirm chrome://webrtc-internals shows relay candidate pairs only

Pass criteria + screenshots template included so future-self can re-verify after VPS changes.

title: Implement livekit-token issuance (validate Supabase JWT, mint LiveKit JWT)
description:
Inside supabase/functions/livekit-token/index.ts:
- Read Authorization: Bearer <supabase JWT> from request
- Validate via createClient(SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON, { global: { headers: { Authorization: ... } } }).auth.getUser()
- If invalid -> 401
- Read room_id from body (default to ROOM_CONFIG.DEFAULT_ROOM_ID for backwards compat; later sbc-* B6 will use room-scoped IDs)
- Use livekit-server-sdk AccessToken to mint a JWT with grants { roomJoin: true, room: 'room-' + room_id, canPublish: true, canSubscribe: true }
- TTL 10 minutes (600s)
- Return { token, url: LIVEKIT_WS_URL }

Done: deployed; auth'd caller gets a token; unauth'd caller gets 401.

title: Replace AudioControls mute/unmute with useLocalParticipant
description:
Today src/components/AudioControls.tsx toggles a MediaStream track on the legacy WebRTCManager. Replace with @livekit/components-react useLocalParticipant().localParticipant.setMicrophoneEnabled(boolean). Mirror state into existing Zustand User.audioEnabled for the rest of the UI.

Behind VITE_USE_LIVEKIT flag (sbc-* B4_5). Legacy path stays until B4_16.

title: Build <RecordUpdate> component using MediaRecorder
description:
src/components/RecordUpdate.tsx. Acquires getUserMedia({ video: true, audio: true }), instantiates MediaRecorder. Exposes start/stop/pause/resume + ondataavailable. Emits a Blob on stop.

Codec preference: video/webm;codecs=vp9,opus -> video/webm;codecs=vp8,opus -> video/mp4 (Safari fallback). Use MediaRecorder.isTypeSupported() to pick.

Standalone — does not depend on anything else.

title: LiveKit connection-state UI: disconnected/reconnecting banner
description:
LiveKit room emits RoomEvent.ConnectionStateChanged ('connecting'|'connected'|'reconnecting'|'disconnected'). Show a non-blocking banner at top of <Room /> when state !== 'connected'. Auto-dismisses on reconnect.

Why: silent reconnects look like 'is anyone there?' to cohort. Two-line UI prevents the bad time.

title: Chunked upload to Supabase Storage with progress UI
description:
supabase.storage.from('recordings').upload(path, blob, { upsert: false, contentType }). Show progress bar (use the upload's onUploadProgress where available). On success, call back with the public/signed URL.

Path: <user_id>/<crypto.randomUUID()>.webm.

title: Cleanup signaling channel in WebRTCManager.disconnect()
description:
Add to disconnect(): await supabase.removeChannel(this.signalingChannel) and null the field. Prevents channel leaks across login/logout cycles.

Done: removeChannel called; verified by leaving + rejoining 3x without dangling channel warnings in supabase realtime debug logs.

title: Integration: theme switching renders correct material colors
description: Vitest with @react-three/test-renderer (or a canvas snapshot). Mount <CircularRoom theme={forest} />, traverse the scene, assert wall mesh material.color.getHexString() matches theme.palette.walls.

title: Scaffold Supabase Edge Function 'livekit-token' + secrets
description:
Create supabase/functions/livekit-token/index.ts. Initial scaffold returns 200 with a stub response. Configure secrets:
- LIVEKIT_API_KEY
- LIVEKIT_API_SECRET
- LIVEKIT_WS_URL=wss://vpsmikewolf.duckdns.org

Run supabase functions deploy livekit-token; verify reachable via fetch.

Document deploy/rollback process in supabase/functions/README.md.

title: Build <LiveKitRoomProvider> component (token fetch + connect)
description:
src/components/LiveKitRoomProvider.tsx wraps @livekit/components-react LiveKitRoom. Fetches token via supabase.functions.invoke('livekit-token', { body: { room_id } }). Connects to wss URL returned. Exposes children + audio/video tracks via LiveKit context.

Mount inside <Room /> when VITE_USE_LIVEKIT is set.

title: fresh test B
Migration: backfill seeded room + members + add room_id to user_updates/sessions
Apply theme palette + lighting + environment preset to <CircularRoom>
description: src/components/CircularRoom.tsx reads the active theme. Replace hardcoded materials/lighting with theme-driven values. Environment preset comes from theme.lighting.environmentPreset.

title: RLS regression: user A cannot delete user B's recording
description:
pgTAP test or a Node script that:
- Auths as user A, uploads a file
- Auths as user B, attempts DELETE -> expects 403
- Asserts file still present after the failed delete

Add to a new tests/storage/ directory.

title: Lobby + Room loading and error states
description:
Cover the boring-but-important cases:

Lobby:
- Loading skeleton while rooms list fetches.
- Empty state (no rooms): primary CTA = 'Create your first room'.
- Error state (network/RLS): retry button + helpful copy.

Room entry:
- 'Joining...' state while LiveKit token + connect happens (don't show empty 3D scene).
- Error: token mint failed, LiveKit connect failed, room missing/forbidden -> banner with 'Return to lobby' button.
- Forbidden (RLS rejected SELECT): show 'You don't have access to this room' + back-to-lobby.

Touch: src/components/Lobby.tsx, src/components/Room.tsx.

title: Existing-cohort onboarding: multi-room tour update + lobby banner
description:
When multi-room ships, existing cohort users land in the seeded 'Second Breakfast Circle' room as before (sbc-29g backfill). But there's now a Lobby and the option to create new rooms.

Scope: dismissible banner on Lobby ('New: rooms with themes!'), append tour step to TOUR_STEPS in src/config/index.ts, surface a one-time 'What's new' modal for users with hasTakenTour=true.

title: Thumbnail generator (canvas snapshot at t=1s)
description: Given a recorded Blob: load into hidden <video>, seek to 1s, draw to canvas, toBlob('image/jpeg', 0.85). Upload as <user_id>/<recording_id>-thumb.jpg via the same Storage flow.

title: Room-scoped Supabase presence (filter roster by current room)
description: useStore subscribes to presence keyed by room_id. Roster panel and 3D avatars only show users in the active room. Channel name: 'room:' + currentRoomId.

title: Gate webrtc sends on subscribe() readiness via ready promise
description:
Race condition: connectToUser() can fire before subscribe() resolves, dropping early offers. Solve via private ready: Promise<void> resolved when status === 'SUBSCRIBED'. Each send awaits this.ready first.

Done: the ready promise is awaited inside the three send paths; integration smoke test (sbc-6) confirms first peer connection no longer needs a retry to succeed.

title: E2E: third tab joins; all three see two remote audio tracks
description: Extends B4_13: third browser context joins. Assert all three contexts report remoteParticipants size == 2 with audio tracks subscribed.

title: Unit: theme registry fallback for unknown theme
description: vitest. resolveTheme('does-not-exist') returns the 'default' theme + console.warn. resolveTheme('forest') returns the forest theme.

title: E2E: record 5s clip, upload, reload, playback
description: Playwright spec using fake media stream (--use-fake-ui-for-media-stream + --use-fake-device-for-media-stream + --use-file-for-fake-video-capture=fixture.y4m). Records 5s, uploads, reloads, asserts <video> element renders with the uploaded URL and currentTime can advance >0.

title: Render <video controls> in UpdateDialog when recording_url present
description: Extend src/components/UpdateDialog.tsx: when update.recording_url is non-null, show a <video controls poster={thumbnail_url} src={recording_url} preload='metadata' /> above (or instead of) the text body.

title: Server-side recording size + duration cap (Storage policy)
description:
Prevent abuse: 1) RLS check on user_updates insert: recording_duration_seconds <= 300; 2) Storage bucket file size cap via Supabase dashboard (max 50MB per file; rejects bigger uploads at storage layer).

Document in supabase/migrations/<ts>_recording_caps.sql + supabase/README.md.

Done: 60MB clip upload fails with a clear error; recording_duration_seconds=400 insert rejected.

title: Delete src/lib/webrtc.ts and VITE_USE_LIVEKIT flag once parity verified
description:
After E2E suites in B4_13/14/15 + manual cohort soak pass, remove:
- src/lib/webrtc.ts
- All WebRTCManager references in src/store/useStore.ts
- VITE_USE_LIVEKIT plumbing in src/config/index.ts and consumers
- Legacy STUN/TURN env vars (if not also used by anything else; LiveKit handles its own TURN)

Confirm npm run build still clean; E2E suite still green.

title: Render remote video as Three.js videoTexture above each chair
description:
When a remote participant's videoEnabled is true, attach their VideoTrack's mediaStreamTrack to a hidden HTMLVideoElement, wrap in THREE.VideoTexture, apply to a small floating plane (~0.4x0.3m) above the avatar sphere in src/components/Participants.tsx.

Hide the plane when video is off. Reuse video element across track changes (don't leak).

title: pgTAP RLS regression suite for rooms + room_members
description:
supabase/tests/rooms_rls.sql using pgTAP. Cases:
- non-member cannot SELECT a private room
- non-member CAN SELECT a public room
- member SELECT works for any visibility once joined
- non-admin cannot UPDATE rooms
- public-room self-join works
- private-room self-join is blocked

title: <Lobby /> component: list rooms + create button
description:
src/components/Lobby.tsx. Sections:
- 'Your rooms' (joined via room_members)
- 'Public rooms' (visibility='public', not joined)
- 'Owned by you' (created_by = currentUser.id)

Each room card shows name, theme swatch, member count, num_chairs. Click sets currentRoomId in Zustand store and mounts <Room />.

'Create room' button opens the modal in B6_5.

title: Replace all 3 webrtc.ts send() callsites with this.signalingChannel.send()
description:
Depends on storage refactor (sbc-1). Three callsites in src/lib/webrtc.ts:
1. onicecandidate (line ~74): supabase.channel('webrtc-signaling').send(...)
2. onnegotiationneeded (line ~99): supabase.channel('webrtc-signaling').send(...)
3. handleSignalingMessage answer path (line ~153): supabase.channel('webrtc-signaling').send(...)

Replace each with this.signalingChannel.send(...). No payload changes.

Done: grep 'supabase.channel' src/lib/webrtc.ts returns zero hits in send paths.

title: Room-scoped talking stick state
description: Whatever table holds the stick state (or its in-memory equivalent), key by room_id. A stick can only be passed between users in the same room. Check in passStick action; reject cross-room passes.

title: fresh test A
E2E: video toggle publishes + remote receives
description: Two browser contexts. User A toggles camera on. Assert User B's context sees a remote video track with track.mediaStreamTrack.readyState === 'live' and non-zero videoWidth/videoHeight on the bound <video> element within 5s.

title: <Room /> accepts active room from store; passes through
description: Refactor src/components/Room.tsx to accept the active room object via store. Pass roomId (and theme) down to <CircularRoom>, <Participants>, audio/video providers.

title: VITE_DEBUG_LOG flag plumbing + .env.example entry
description: .env.example: VITE_DEBUG_LOG=  (empty by default). README documents valid values.

title: Storage cleanup: delete recording file when user_update is deleted
description:
When a user_updates row with recording_url is deleted, the associated Storage file in the 'recordings' bucket would otherwise be orphaned (storage cost + privacy issue).

Two viable approaches:
1. Postgres trigger that calls supabase.storage delete via PostgREST/pg_net — complex but transactional.
2. Application-side: in the delete-update flow, await a Storage remove() before the DB delete.

Recommendation: ship #2 in client code AND add a periodic edge function (cron) that reaps storage files whose path doesn't appear in any user_updates.recording_url. Belt and suspenders.

Done: deleting an update that has a recording removes both the row and the file. Manual test: delete, then GET the URL -> 404.

title: E2E: invite room create + join + visibility
description: Playwright. User A creates an invite-only room. User B accepts the link, lands inside. User C (no link) opens the lobby and does NOT see the room in any list.

title: Unit tests for livekit-token edge function
description:
Tests run locally with supabase functions serve. Cases:
- No Authorization header -> 401
- Bad JWT -> 401
- Valid JWT -> 200 with { token, url }; decode token; assert grants include roomJoin and the expected room name
- Token expiry exactly 600s

Use Deno's std/testing or a small vitest harness via supabase test runner.

title: Migration: 'recordings' Storage bucket + RLS policies
description:
supabase/migrations/<ts>_recordings_bucket.sql:
- INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public) VALUES ('recordings', 'recordings', false) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;
- RLS on storage.objects scoped to bucket_id = 'recordings':
  - SELECT: authenticated users can read all
  - INSERT: auth.uid() = (storage.foldername(name))[1]::uuid (path = '<user_id>/<recording_id>.webm')
  - UPDATE/DELETE: same owner check

Document expected layout: <user_id>/<recording_id>.webm + <user_id>/<recording_id>-thumb.jpg.

title: Add VITE_USE_LIVEKIT feature flag
description:
Plumb a flag through src/config/index.ts so we can ship LiveKit alongside the legacy WebRTC and switch atomically. Default false during dev, flip to true once parity verified (sbc-* B4_16 deletes the flag and the legacy code).

Touch: src/config/index.ts; consumer in src/store/useStore.ts (chooses transport).

title: Wire <Lobby /> as gate before <Room /> in App.tsx
description: src/App.tsx: after auth, render <Lobby /> if currentRoomId is null; render <Room roomId={currentRoomId} /> otherwise. 'Leave room' in Room.tsx clears currentRoomId, returning to lobby.

title: Invite landing route: insert room_member + redirect
description: URL pattern: /join/<slug>?token=<jwt>. Frontend route validates the token (call a verification edge function or verify locally if shipping JWT_SECRET to the client is unacceptable -> separate verify endpoint). On valid token + authenticated user: insert into room_members (member), set currentRoomId, redirect into room.

title: Replace remote-track subscription in useStore with useTracks
description:
src/store/useStore.ts currently glues WebRTCManager remote-stream callbacks into User state. Switch to listening on LiveKit's RoomEvent.TrackSubscribed / TrackUnsubscribed (via useTracks in a hook component) and updating per-user audioEnabled/videoEnabled flags.

Behind flag.

title: Add TURN servers to iceServers in createPeerConnection
Debug toggle: iceTransportPolicy='relay' for TURN-only testing
description:
Add VITE_FORCE_RELAY env flag. When set, override iceTransportPolicy from 'all' to 'relay'. Lets us prove the TURN path works in isolation.

Touch: src/lib/webrtc.ts.

title: Add video-publishing toggle to AudioControls
description:
New camera-on/off button next to mic. Calls localParticipant.setCameraEnabled(true/false). Mirror into User.videoEnabled. Keep mic and camera as independent toggles.

Touch: src/components/AudioControls.tsx.

title: Refactor WebRTCManager to hold a single subscribed signaling channel
description:
Current bug: every send() in src/lib/webrtc.ts calls supabase.channel('webrtc-signaling') which returns a new unsubscribed channel; broadcasts are silently dropped, peers never connect.

Fix: Add private signalingChannel: RealtimeChannel field. Initialize once in setupSignaling(). Awaited subscribe() must report SUBSCRIBED status before resolving setup.

Touch: src/lib/webrtc.ts only. ~30 lines.

Why this lands even though LiveKit (sbc-* feature 4) supersedes it: LiveKit migration is multi-day. Cohort needs working live audio in the meantime. Trivial cost, high cohort value during migration window.

Done: WebRTCManager has a single channel field; setupSignaling() awaits SUBSCRIBED before returning; no behavior changes yet to send() callsites (those are next bead).

title: Room-scoped LiveKit room name ('room-<id>')
description: Update livekit-token edge function (sbc-* B4_3) and the LiveKitRoomProvider (sbc-* B4_6): use 'room-' + currentRoomId instead of the hardcoded constant. Coordinate with B4_6 — if B6 lands first, ship as a config knob; if B4 lands first, this is a one-liner update.

title: Apply theme seat colors to <Participants>
description: src/components/Participants.tsx: chair material colors switch from hardcoded #4a5568 to theme.palette.seatPrimary / seatSecondary.

title: Codec detection + Safari fallback messaging
description: If no supported codec, render a banner: 'Your browser does not support video recording. Try Chrome or Firefox.' Don't crash. Feature-detect MediaRecorder existence first.

title: Edge function: invite-link token (signed JWT with room_id + expires_at)
description:
supabase/functions/room-invite/index.ts. POST { room_id } -> issues a signed JWT (HS256 with INVITE_SECRET) embedding { room_id, expires_at } (default 7 days). Caller must be an admin/owner of the room.

Rate limit per user. Validate room exists.

title: Use signed URLs for private 'recordings' bucket playback
description:
The recordings bucket is private (sbc-3cx). user_updates.recording_url cannot be a public URL — playback would 403.

Two options:
1. Store the storage path (not URL) in user_updates.recording_url. Each playback call createSignedUrl(path, 3600).
2. Long-lived signed URL at insert time (e.g. 7-day) — simpler client, but URLs leak in DB.

Recommendation: option 1. Helper hook useRecordingSignedUrl(path) in src/lib/recordings.ts that caches signed URLs in memory keyed by path.

Touch: src/lib/recordings.ts (new), src/components/UpdateDialog.tsx (use the hook).

Done: closed bucket; auth'd user can play; non-auth or other-bucket access returns 403.

title: Two-tab WebRTC smoke script (scripts/smoke-webrtc.ts)
description:
Headless or headful Playwright script: opens two tabs against localhost:5173, signs in two test users, navigates into the room, asserts each tab's RTCPeerConnection.connectionState reaches 'connected' within 10 seconds.

Used as the regression check for the interim WebRTC fix. Will also serve as a 'we deleted LiveKit and broke audio' canary if invoked manually.

title: Wire LiveKit token-refresh callback (10min expiry)
description:
LiveKit client supports connectOptions.tokenRefresh (or onTokenExpired event). Wire to re-call the edge function ~30s before expiry to keep long sessions alive.

Done: 15min co-working session does not disconnect at the 10min mark.

title: test bead
docs/SMOKE.md manual checklist
description: Markdown checklist with sections per feature (1 through 6). Each section: prerequisites, steps, pass criteria, screenshots template. Goal: a non-author can verify the system end-to-end in <30 minutes.

title: Slug generation utility (unicode-safe + collision append)
description:
src/lib/slug.ts. Pure function: name -> slug. Lowercase, NFKD-normalize, strip combining marks, replace non-[a-z0-9] runs with '-', trim. On collision (UNIQUE violation), append -2/-3/... up to -99.

Used by B6_5.

title: E2E: two browsers, audio publish/subscribe parity
description: Playwright spec: two browser contexts, two test users sign in, both join the room, both unmute. Assert each context's LiveKit room.remoteParticipants size == 1 with audio track. Audio level events fire on both sides within 5s of mic open.

title: Theme registry: src/config/themes.ts (4 starter themes)
description:
Closed-set typed registry. Each theme:
{ id, displayName, palette: { ambient, walls, floor, accent, seatPrimary, seatSecondary }, lighting: { ambientIntensity, pointIntensity, environmentPreset }, geometry: { wallStyle, floorPattern } }

Themes:
- default (sunset, current look)
- forest (warm green, dawn preset)
- ocean (cool blue, night preset)
- library (warm amber, warehouse preset)

Independent of schema migrations.

title: Roster panel '🎥' indicator for unread video updates
description: src/components/RosterPanel.tsx: when a user's hasUnreadUpdates is true AND their latest update has recording_url, render the camera glyph next to their name. Falls back to existing dot indicator otherwise.

title: Two-browser fixture for sync tests
description:
tests/fixtures/twoBrowsers.ts: helper that spawns two browser contexts, signs each into a distinct test user, and returns { pageA, pageB, cleanup }.

Used by sbc-* B4_13/14/15 and B6_19.

title: Add LiveKit deps + measure bundle-size baseline
description:
Add to package.json (dependencies):
- livekit-client (latest stable, ~1.x)
- @livekit/components-react
- @livekit/components-styles
And devDependencies (or dependencies if used in edge fn locally):
- livekit-server-sdk (used by Supabase Edge Function in sbc-* B4_2)

Run npm run build before/after; record gzip bundle size delta in the bead's comments. Budget: +200KB gzip. If exceeded, investigate code-split.

Done: deps installed; size delta recorded.

title: Wire logger calls into webrtc.ts, livekit code, recording flow
description:
After src/lib/logger.ts (sbc-1zo) lands, swap raw console.log/console.error in:
- src/lib/webrtc.ts (namespace: 'webrtc')
- src/components/LiveKitRoomProvider.tsx + AudioControls audio paths (namespace: 'livekit')
- src/components/RecordUpdate.tsx + upload code (namespace: 'recording')
- src/store/useStore.ts presence + room sections (namespace: 'presence', 'room')

Keeps console clean for cohort users; verbose mode (VITE_DEBUG_LOG=*) restores full trace for debugging.

title: .env.example + README docs for VITE_TURN_URL/USERNAME/CREDENTIAL
description:
Add VITE_TURN_URL, VITE_TURN_USERNAME, VITE_TURN_CREDENTIAL to .env.example with example values pointing at vpsmikewolf.duckdns.org. README section 'Network requirements' explains why TURN is needed (symmetric NAT, hotel wifi) and where the canonical credentials live.

Touch: .env.example, README.md.
